volumeTitle: The Merchant. The Braggart Soldier. The Ghost. The Persian
label: The Ghost (Section 4.5.1032b - Section 5.1.1097d)

sections.label: Section 4.5.1032b
sections.translation: Yes ; he has thrown everything into confusion . He has made a fool of me to-day in a disgraceful manner .

sections.label: Section 4.5.1034
sections.translation: What is it you say ?

sections.label: Section 4.5.1034b
sections.original: Haec res sic est ut narro tibi :
sections.translation: This matter is just as I am telling you :

sections.label: Section 4.5.1035
sections.original: deludificatust me hodie in perpetuom modum .
sections.translation: he has this day made a fool of me in an outrageous manner . Now I beseech you that you ’ ll kindly aid me , and lend me your assistance .

sections.label: Section 4.5.1037
sections.translation: What would you have ?

sections.label: Section 4.5.1037b
sections.original: I mecum hinc , obsecro , ad te una simul .
sections.translation: I beg of you , come this way together with me .

sections.label: Section 4.5.1038
sections.translation: Be it so .

sections.label: Section 4.5.1038b
sections.original: Servorumque operam et lora mihi cedo .
sections.translation: Lend me the assistance of your slaves and some scourges .

sections.label: Section 4.5.1039
sections.translation: Take them by all means .

sections.label: Section 4.5.1039b
sections.translation: At the same time I ’ ll tell you about this ,

sections.label: Section 4.5.1040
sections.original: quis med exemplis hodie ludificatus est .
sections.translation: in what a fashion he has this day imposed upon me .

sections.label: Section 5.1.1041
sections.original: Quí homo timidus erit in rebus dubiis , nauci non erit ;
sections.translation: The man that shall prove timid in critical matters , will not be worth a nutshell . And , really , to say what that expression , worth a nutshell , means , I don ’ t know . But after my master sent me into the country to fetch his son hither ,

sections.label: Section 5.1.1044_1045
sections.original: abii illac per angiportum ad hortum nostrum clanculum ,
sections.translation: I went that way ( pointing ) slily through the lane to our garden . At the entrance to the garden that ’ s in the lane , I opened the door ; and by that road I led out all the troop , both men and women . After , from being in a state of siege , I had led out my troops to a place of safety , I adopted the plan of convoking a senate of my comrades ,

sections.label: Section 5.1.1050
sections.original: quoniam convocavi , atque illi me ex senatu segregant .
sections.translation: and when I had convoked it , they forthwith banished me from the senate . When I myself perceived that the matter must be decided by my own judgment , as soon as ever I could , I did the same as many others do , whose affairs are in a critical or a perplexed state ; they proceed to render them more perplexed , so that nothing can be settled .

sections.label: Section 5.1.1054_1055
sections.original: nam scio equidem nullo pacto iam esse posse haec clam senem .
sections.translation: But I know full well , that now by no means can this be concealed from the old man .

sections.label: Section 5.1.1062
sections.original: sed quid hoc ést , quod foris concrepuit proxima vicinia ?
sections.translation: But how ’ s this , that our next neighbour ’ s door makes a noise ? Why , surely this is my master : I ’ d like to have a taste of his talk .

sections.label: Section 5.1.1064
sections.original: Ilico intra limen isti astate , ut , cum extemplo vocem ,
sections.translation: Do you stand there , in that spot within the threshold ; so that , the very instant I call ,

sections.label: Section 5.1.1065
sections.translation: you may sally forth at once . Quickly fasten the handcuffs upon him . I ’ ll wait before the house for this fellow that makes a fool of me , whose hide I ’ ll make a fool of in fine style , if I live .

sections.label: Section 5.1.1068
sections.original: Res palam est . nunc te videre meliust quid agas , Tranio .
sections.translation: The affair ’ s all out . Now it ’ s best for you , Tranio , to consider what you are to do .

sections.label: Section 5.1.1069
sections.original: Docte atque astu mihi captandumst cum illo , ubi huc advenerit .
sections.translation: I must go to work to catch him cleverly and artfully when he comes here .

sections.label: Section 5.1.1070
sections.original: non ego illi éxtemplo hamum ostendam , sensim mittam lineam .
sections.translation: I ’ ll not disclose to him my feelings all at once ; I ’ ll throw out my line ; I ’ ll conceal the fact that I know anything of these matters .

sections.label: Section 5.1.1071b
sections.translation: O cunning mortal ! not another person in Athens can be pronounced more clever than he . One can no more this day deceive him than he can a stone . I ’ ll accost the man ; I ’ ll address him .

sections.label: Section 5.1.1074b
sections.original: Nunc ego ille húc veniat velim .
sections.translation: Now I do wish that he would come here .

sections.label: Section 5.1.1075
sections.original: Siquidem pol me quaeris , adsum praesens praesenti tibi .
sections.translation: I ’ faith , if me indeed you want , here I am ready at hand for you . ( Comes forward .)

sections.label: Section 5.1.1076
sections.translation: Bravo ! Tranio , what ’ s being done ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.1076b
sections.translation: The country people are coming from the country Philolaches will be here in a moment .

sections.label: Section 5.1.1077b
sections.translation: I ’ faith , he comes opportunely for me . This neighbour of ours I take to be a shameless and dishonest fellow .

sections.label: Section 5.1.1079
sections.translation: Why so ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.1079b
sections.translation: Inasmuch as he denies that he knows you .

sections.label: Section 5.1.1079c
sections.translation: Denies it ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.1079d
sections.translation: And declares that you never gave him a single coin of money

sections.label: Section 5.1.1080b
sections.original: Abi , lúdis me , credo haud negat .
sections.translation: Out with you , you are joking me , I do believe ; he doesn ’ t deny it .

sections.label: Section 5.1.1081
sections.translation: How so ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.1081b
sections.original: Scio , iocaris nunc tu . nam ille quidem edepol haud negat .
sections.translation: I am sure now that you are joking ; for surely he doesn ’ t deny it .

sections.label: Section 5.1.1082
sections.original: Immo edepol negat profecto , neque se hasce aedis Philolachi
sections.translation: Nay but , upon my faith , he really does deny it ; or that he has sold this house to Philolaches .

sections.label: Section 5.1.1083b
sections.original: Eho , an negavit sibi datum argentum , obsecro ?
sections.translation: Well now , pray , has he denied that the money was paid him ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.1084
sections.original: Quin ius iurandum pollicitust dare se , si vellem , mihi ,
sections.translation: Nay more , he offered to take an oath to me , if I desired it ,

sections.label: Section 5.1.1085
sections.original: neque se hasce aedis vendidisse neque sibi argentum datum est .
sections.translation: that he had neither sold this house , nor had any money him paid been .

sections.label: Section 5.1.1087
sections.translation: I told him the same that you told me .

sections.label: Section 5.1.1087b
sections.translation: What did he say ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.1087c
sections.translation: He offered to give up all his servants for examination .

sections.label: Section 5.1.1088b
sections.translation: Nonsense ! On my faith , he never will give them up .

sections.label: Section 5.1.1089
sections.translation: He really does offer them .

sections.label: Section 5.1.1089b
sections.original: Quin i cum illo in ius . sine inveniam .
sections.translation: Why then , do you summon him to trial .

sections.label: Section 5.1.1089c
sections.translation: Wait a bit ;

sections.label: Section 5.1.1090
sections.translation: I ’ ll make trial as I fancy . I ’ m determined on it .

sections.label: Section 5.1.1090b
sections.translation: Bring the fellow here to me .

sections.label: Section 5.1.1091
sections.original: vel hominem iube aedis † mancipio poscere .
sections.translation: or else bid the young man to demand possession of the house .

sections.label: Section 5.1.1091b
sections.translation: Why no , I want to do this first — to put the servants under examination [*] .

sections.label: Section 5.1.1092b
sections.translation: I ’ faith , I think it ought to be done .

sections.label: Section 5.1.1093
sections.original: Quid si igitur ego accersam homines ?
sections.translation: What then , if I go fetch some men ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.1093b
sections.translation: It ought to have been done already ;

sections.label: Section 5.1.1094
sections.translation: Meantime , I ’ ll take possession of this altar [*] .

sections.label: Section 5.1.1094b
sections.translation: Why so ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.1094c
sections.translation: You can understand nothing .

sections.label: Section 5.1.1095
sections.original: né enim illi huc confugere possint , quaestioni quos dabit .
sections.translation: Why , that those may not be able to take refuge here whom he shall give up for examination , I ’ ll keep guard here for you ; so that the examination may not come to nothing .

sections.label: Section 5.1.1097
sections.translation: Get up from the altar .

sections.label: Section 5.1.1097b
sections.translation: By no means .

sections.label: Section 5.1.1097c
sections.translation: Prithee , don ’ t you take possession of the altar .

sections.label: Section 5.1.1097d
sections.translation: Why so ?
